forecasts.reasoning: The knowledge cutoff is April 25, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025—a very short window of about two months (67 days). Changes to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) are relatively infrequent; historically, there have been around 50–60 changes in roughly 130 years (about once every 2+ years on average). While changes can cluster, the DJIA just underwent a restructuring in November 2024 (adding Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams) and February 2024 (adding Amazon). It is uncommon for index providers to alter the composition again so soon after a rebalance without a forcing event like a huge corporate action (e.g. going private, monumental stock split affecting price weightings). None of the 30 companies on the current list are in imminent danger of being acquired, and despite recent price volatility due to tariffs (with Nike and Caterpillar falling heavily in early April 2025), their share prices and market positions remain far above the historic thresholds that prompt ejection (such as Intel or Walgreens dropping to the low $20s and suffering structural core business decline). The baseline probability of a change occurring in any given 2-month period is less than 10%, and given the recent rebalancing, an immediate subsequent change appears even less likely. Therefore, I assign a 6% probability to an official announcement of a DJIA component removal before July 1, 2025.

The knowledge cutoff is April 25, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025. This leaves a forecasting horizon of roughly 67 days (about 18% of a year). Historically, Africa and Latin America see an average of ~1–1.5 successful coups per year. During the 2020–2023 intense 'wave' of coups in Africa, the rate approached 2.5 per year, but there have been no successful coups since Gabon in August 2023 (a gap of over 20 months), suggesting a regression to the mean. Extrapolating a base rate of 1 successful coup per year would mean an ~18% chance of one occurring over the next 67 days. Without any identified imminent, specific coup plots in the days immediately prior to the cutoff, I am adjusting slightly below the statistical base rate to 14%.
